body:
status=open
reason=Host-level disk exhaustion (ENOSPC) makes the Bash tool completely unusable in this execution worktree — every invocation fails before running any command, while trying to mkdir its own session temp directory under /tmp/claude-501/...: "ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-480054d5-20260708T175723-6db85ea6'". This reproduced identically across direct Bash calls (with and without dangerouslyDisableSandbox) and inside a fresh general-purpose subagent, confirming it is a host/tmp-filesystem capacity problem, not something scoped to this bead's code. Because Bash cannot start, `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/...`, `lefthook run pre-commit`, and even `git add`/`git commit` cannot be run from this worktree. Read/Write/Edit tools (which do not depend on the /tmp session directory) continued to work normally, so the source and test edits below were made but are uncommitted. Source changes already applied in this worktree (uncommitted, ready for a retry to verify and commit once Bash/tmp is available again): 1. cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go - Added `DefaultTerminalBlockTTL = 10 * time.Minute` to the const block (alongside defaultRestartBackoff/defaultRestartLimit). - Added `expireStaleTerminalBlocksLocked(now time.Time)` method: deletes blockedTerminals entries whose TerminalAt is older than `now - DefaultTerminalBlockTTL`, regardless of how desired.UpdatedAt compares to the terminal timestamp. - `canStartMore` now calls `s.expireStaleTerminalBlocksLocked(now)` as the first statement after acquiring the lock, before the existing dirty-worktree and `clearResolvedTerminalBlocksLocked(state.UpdatedAt)` freshness checks (both of which are left intact). 2. cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go - Added `TestWorkerSupervisor_ExpiredBlockedTerminalDoesNotSuppressRestartForever` with two subtests: - "block older than TTL expires even without desired-state refresh": seeds an operator_attention terminal at `now - DefaultTerminalBlockTTL - 1m` with `desired.UpdatedAt` set *before* the terminal (so the existing freshness-clear path would NOT fire pre-fix), then asserts `ReconcileAt(now)` reaches 1 running worker. - "block younger than TTL still suppresses restart": same setup but terminal at `now - DefaultTerminalBlockTTL + 1m` (i.e. within the TTL window), asserts the worker count stays 0. Verification commands to run once the environment has free disk space / working Bash (repo cwd is the worktree root): cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestWorkerSupervisor_ExpiredBlockedTerminalDoesNotSuppressRestartForever" -v cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... lefthook run pre-commit If all three pass, stage exactly: git add cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go and commit with a subject ending in [ddx-480054d5]. No destructive host remediation (e.g. clearing /tmp, deleting other worktrees) was attempted from this session since that is shared infrastructure outside this bead's scope and outside what an isolated bead-execution agent should do unilaterally.
suggested_action=decompose into executable follow-up work or retry with a stronger agent

no_changes
rationale: status: blocked
reason: Host-level disk exhaustion (ENOSPC) makes the Bash tool completely unusable in this execution worktree — every invocation fails before running any command, while trying to mkdir its own session temp directory under /tmp/claude-501/...: "ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-480054d5-20260708T175723-6db85ea6'". This reproduced identically across direct Bash calls (with and without dangerouslyDisableSandbox) and inside a fresh general-purpose subagent, confirming it is a host/tmp-filesystem capacity problem, not something scoped to this bead's code. Because Bash cannot start, `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/...`, `lefthook run pre-commit`, and even `git add`/`git commit` cannot be run from this worktree. Read/Write/Edit tools (which do not depend on the /tmp session directory) continued to work normally, so the source and test edits below were made but are uncommitted.

Source changes already applied in this worktree (uncommitted, ready for a retry to verify and commit once Bash/tmp is available again):

1. cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go
   - Added `DefaultTerminalBlockTTL = 10 * time.Minute` to the const block (alongside defaultRestartBackoff/defaultRestartLimit).
   - Added `expireStaleTerminalBlocksLocked(now time.Time)` method: deletes blockedTerminals entries whose TerminalAt is older than `now - DefaultTerminalBlockTTL`, regardless of how desired.UpdatedAt compares to the terminal timestamp.
   - `canStartMore` now calls `s.expireStaleTerminalBlocksLocked(now)` as the first statement after acquiring the lock, before the existing dirty-worktree and `clearResolvedTerminalBlocksLocked(state.UpdatedAt)` freshness checks (both of which are left intact).

2. cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go
   - Added `TestWorkerSupervisor_ExpiredBlockedTerminalDoesNotSuppressRestartForever` with two subtests:
     - "block older than TTL expires even without desired-state refresh": seeds an operator_attention terminal at `now - DefaultTerminalBlockTTL - 1m` with `desired.UpdatedAt` set *before* the terminal (so the existing freshness-clear path would NOT fire pre-fix), then asserts `ReconcileAt(now)` reaches 1 running worker.
     - "block younger than TTL still suppresses restart": same setup but terminal at `now - DefaultTerminalBlockTTL + 1m` (i.e. within the TTL window), asserts the worker count stays 0.

Verification commands to run once the environment has free disk space / working Bash (repo cwd is the worktree root):
  cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestWorkerSupervisor_ExpiredBlockedTerminalDoesNotSuppressRestartForever" -v
  cd cli && go test ./internal/server/...
  lefthook run pre-commit

If all three pass, stage exactly:
  git add cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go
and commit with a subject ending in [ddx-480054d5].

No destructive host remediation (e.g. clearing /tmp, deleting other worktrees) was attempted from this session since that is shared infrastructure outside this bead's scope and outside what an isolated bead-execution agent should do unilaterally.

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"system_unready: Description gives PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE with file:line (workers_supervisor.go:411-418, 441-453), a concrete PROPOSED FIX (add DefaultTerminalBlockTTL=10m, expire stale blockedTerminals entries independent of desired.UpdatedAt freshness), and an explicit NON-SCOPE list with parent and no-deps stated. AC is numbered, names a specific Test* function with both required behaviors (expiry after TTL, suppression retained before TTL), a go test -run filter, a full package test command, and the lefthook gate. The single prior_attempts entry is a no_changes outcome caused by host-level ENOSPC/tmp exhaustion in that worktree, not a code or design blocker — Read/Write/Edit still worked and the agent successfully drafted the fix and test, it just couldn't run Bash to verify/commit. That is an environmental failure orthogonal to this bead's scope and should not block a retry in a healthy worktree.","fingerprint":"c981fccff52826fb0eb5a857cf5ae4ccebbfb7038419d4e86ab7a2236f65f52d","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"system_unready","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"c981fccff52826fb0eb5a857cf5ae4ccebbfb7038419d4e86ab7a2236f65f52d"}
